description:
The logrotate utility allows for the automatic rotation of log files. The frequency of rotation is specified in /etc/logrotate.conf, which triggers a cron task or a timer. To configure logrotate to run daily, add or correct the following line in /etc/logrotate.conf: 
# rotate log files frequency
daily
rationale:
Log files that are not properly rotated run the risk of growing so large
that they fill up the /var/log partition. Valuable logging information could be lost
if the /var/log partition becomes full.
ocil_clause: logrotate is not configured to run daily
ocil:
To determine the status and frequency of logrotate, run the following command: 
$ sudo grep logrotate /var/log/cron*
If logrotate is configured properly, output should include references to /etc/cron.daily.
title: Ensure Logrotate Runs Periodically
